title: Host-Based Routing with Ingress
description:
Route HTTP traffic to different backend services based on the HTTP Host header using a single Ingress resource.

Deploy a `frontend` service (nginx) and a `backend` API service (httpd). Create an Ingress with two host rules: `app.example.com` → frontend, `api.example.com` → backend.

**Common Pitfalls**: Each hostname needs its own entry in `spec.rules`. Omitting `host` makes the rule a wildcard matching any hostname. Test locally with `curl -H 'Host: app.example.com' http://<ingress-ip>/`.
steps: Create a Deployment named frontend-deploy using image nginx:alpine
Create a ClusterIP Service named frontend-service on port 80 selecting frontend-deploy pods
Create a Deployment named backend-deploy using image httpd:alpine
Create a ClusterIP Service named backend-service on port 80 selecting backend-deploy pods
Create an Ingress named host-ingress with two host rules: app.example.com → frontend-service:80 and api.example.com → backend-service:80
Set ingressClassName to nginx and pathType to Prefix with path / for each host rule
hints: Use app: frontend-deploy and app: backend-deploy as Pod labels so Services can select them — matchLabels, template labels, and Service selector must all match
spec.rules is an array — add one item per hostname, each with its own http.paths block
Use path: / with pathType: Prefix to catch all requests for that hostname
kubectl describe ingress host-ingress shows both rules and their backends

editor_config.initial_code:
# Frontend Deployment
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: frontend-deploy
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: # TODO: use 'frontend-deploy'
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: # TODO: use 'frontend-deploy'
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: frontend
        image: # TODO: nginx:alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: frontend-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: frontend-deploy
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80
---
# Backend Deployment
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: backend-deploy
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: # TODO: use 'backend-deploy'
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: # TODO: use 'backend-deploy'
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: backend
        image: # TODO: httpd:alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: backend-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: backend-deploy
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80
---
# Ingress: host-based routing
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: host-ingress
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  rules:
  - host: app.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: frontend-service
            port:
              number: 80
  - host: api.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: backend-service
            port:
              number: 80

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: frontend-deploy
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: frontend-deploy
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: frontend-deploy
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: frontend
        image: nginx:alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: frontend-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: frontend-deploy
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: backend-deploy
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: backend-deploy
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: backend-deploy
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: backend
        image: httpd:alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: backend-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: backend-deploy
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: host-ingress
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  rules:
  - host: app.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: frontend-service
            port:
              number: 80
  - host: api.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: backend-service
            port:
              number: 80
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

kubectl apply -f solution.yaml

kubectl rollout status deployment/frontend-deploy --timeout=60s
kubectl rollout status deployment/backend-deploy --timeout=60s
echo "✓ Both Deployments are ready"

kubectl get service frontend-service > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✓ frontend-service exists" || { echo "✗ frontend-service not found"; exit 1; }
kubectl get service backend-service > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✓ backend-service exists" || { echo "✗ backend-service not found"; exit 1; }

kubectl get ingress host-ingress > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✓ Ingress host-ingress exists" || { echo "✗ Ingress host-ingress not found"; exit 1; }

# Verify two host rules
hosts=$(kubectl get ingress host-ingress -o jsonpath='{.spec.rules[*].host}')
if echo "$hosts" | grep -q "app.example.com" && echo "$hosts" | grep -q "api.example.com"; then
  echo "✓ Both host rules (app.example.com, api.example.com) are defined"
else
  echo "✗ Missing host rules. Found: $hosts"
  exit 1
fi

# Verify backends
backends=$(kubectl get ingress host-ingress -o jsonpath='{.spec.rules[*].http.paths[*].backend.service.name}')
if echo "$backends" | grep -q "frontend-service" && echo "$backends" | grep -q "backend-service"; then
  echo "✓ Both backends (frontend-service, backend-service) are configured"
else
  echo "✗ Missing backends. Found: $backends"
  exit 1
fi

# Verify ingressClassName
class=$(kubectl get ingress host-ingress -o jsonpath='{.spec.ingressClassName}')
if [ "$class" = "nginx" ]; then
  echo "✓ ingressClassName is nginx"
else
  echo "✗ ingressClassName is '$class', expected 'nginx'"
  exit 1
fi

echo "✓ All validations passed"
exit 0
